L1.area: Template engine initialization and extensibility layer

L2.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where template-controlled lookup keys resolve against inherited prototype properties of internal registry objects. By exploiting prototype method resolution during filter evaluation, an attacker can leak the template engine's internal context and object graph, ultimately reaching the Function constructor to execute arbitrary code including OS commands.

L3.area: Template engine class and filter/tag registry initialization
L3.description: A code injection vulnerability exists where the template engine's filter and tag registries are initialized as plain JavaScript objects, causing template-controlled lookup keys to resolve against inherited Object.prototype properties. By exploiting prototype method resolution (such as valueOf or constructor) during filter or tag dispatch, an attacker can leak internal engine state and traverse the object graph to reach the Function constructor, enabling arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The filters and tags registries in the Liquid class are plain {} objects, so template-controlled keys inherit from Object.prototype. Accessing valueOf via a filter leaks the internal context, allowing prototype chain traversal to reach the Function constructor and execute arbitrary OS commands.
